title: selaginella caffrorum var. aethiopica
intro: Selaginella caffrorum var. aethiopica is a species of flowering plant in the Selaginella genus, commonly known as the "false parsley fern". This plant is known to have various medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The health benefits of Selaginella caffrorum var. aethiopica include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce swelling and prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and powdering the leaves, which can be made into teas, infusions, or capsules for medicinal use.
